system.name: Kids on Bikes
system.description: Everyday people encountering strange, scary, and otherworldly forces they can't comprehend, much less overpower.

stats.name: Brains
stats.description: How book-smart a character is. Determines how well they understand problems and solve intellectual puzzles.

stats.name: Brawn
stats.description: How much brute strength a character has. Does not determine fighting ability — just lifting, athleticism, and physical intimidation.

stats.name: Fight
stats.description: How scary a character is in and out of a fight. Determines intimidation and ability to handle physical confrontation.

stats.name: Flight
stats.description: How fast and evasive a character is, both physically and verbally. Quick legs and quick mouths.

stats.name: Charm
stats.description: How socially adept a character is. Reading emotions, talking out of tough situations, persuasion.

stats.name: Grit
stats.description: How hard it is to unsettle a character emotionally or physically. Keeping cool under pressure. Street smarts.

descriptions.d20: Superb — obviously a strength upon first meeting.
descriptions.d12: Impressive — people who know you would say you're pretty good.
descriptions.d10: Above Average — slightly better than most.
descriptions.d8: Below Average — not bad, but not good either.
descriptions.d6: Bad — people who know you would say you're pretty bad.
descriptions.d4: Terrible — obviously a weakness upon first meeting.

ageGroups.name: Adult
ageGroups.description: Seen enough of the world to know what it's about.

tropes.name: Adventurous Scout
questions: Who first got you into Scouting (or the great outdoors)?
What do you have to give up to spend as much time in nature as you do?

tropes.name: Brilliant Mathlete
questions: Why do you get so much satisfaction from your academic excellence?
What have you sacrificed to be so good in school?

tropes.name: Conspiracy Theorist
questions: What are you sure is happening in the town that no one else knows about?
How far will you go to have others believe you?

tropes.name: Cunning Detective
questions: What's the most impressive case you cracked?
What case could you never solve, and why does it still bother you?

tropes.name: Blue-Collar Worker
questions: Though it's tough work, what do you love about what you do?
What would it mean for you if you lost your job?

tropes.name: Daring Athlete
questions: Why do you get so much satisfaction from being excellent at your sport?
What have you sacrificed to be so good in this sport?

tropes.name: Dedicated Farmer
questions: Why does working the land bring you so much joy?
What do you miss out on because of the time you have to put in farming?

tropes.name: Laidback Slacker
questions: What do you think of all the try-hards around you?
What are you willing to go the extra mile for?

tropes.name: Funny Sidekick
questions: What do you do that always lightens your friends' moods?
When does being in the 'sidekick' role frustrate you?

tropes.name: Overeager Enthusiast
questions: How did you get so passionate about what you're so passionate about?
What have you ignored to pursue this passion?

tropes.name: Popular Kid
questions: Beyond people wanting to impress you and the social capital that brings, what do you like about being popular?
How do you treat the unpopular kids?

tropes.name: Prom Royalty
questions: What benefits have you gained from having such a high place in the school's social hierarchy?
How did it feel the first time you heard someone say that you're 'beautiful but terrible'?

tropes.name: Animal Lover
questions: When did you get the pet that's always by your side?
Why do you feel like your pet understands you better than most people?

tropes.name: Mysterious Transfer
questions: Why is being new and mysterious so much fun for you?
Which rumor about you hurts the most?

tropes.name: Overprotective Parent
questions: When do you feel most appreciated by your kid(s)?
What about your kid(s) do you wish you could change?

tropes.name: Unlikely Ally
questions: What makes it so surprising that you're working with this group?
Despite your aloof exterior, what do you genuinely care about?

tropes.name: Aspiring Wannabe
questions: What would it mean to be one of the popular kids?
What would you sacrifice to be one of the cool kids?

tropes.name: Freakazoid
questions: What are the key components of your weirdo facade?
What are you worried people will find out about you if you drop your facade?

tropes.name: Goody Goody
questions: Why does approval from adults mean so much to you?
Who did you recently hurt by seeking an adult's approval?

tropes.name: Silver Spoon
questions: What advantages has your wealth given you?
How does your family exploit the town to make its money?

tropes.name: Reclusive Eccentric
questions: What drove you away from the world at large?
What have you gained from your time away from the world?

tropes.name: Seasoned Babysitter
questions: How did you get your start babysitting?
What's a mistake you made while babysitting that bothers you to this day?

tropes.name: Stoic Professional
questions: To what extent do you enjoy the work you do?
What would you rather be doing?

tropes.name: Young Provider
questions: How has working to support your family improved you as a person?
What have you had to sacrifice to support your family?

strengths.name: Cool Under Pressure
strengths.description: May spend 1 AT to take half die value instead of rolling on a Snap Decision.

strengths.name: Easygoing
strengths.description: Gain 2 Adversity Tokens when you fail, instead of 1.

strengths.name: Gross
strengths.description: You have a gross bodily trick you can do on command.

strengths.name: Heroic
strengths.description: You do not need the GM's permission to spend AT to ignore Fears.

strengths.name: Intuitive
strengths.description: May spend 1 AT to ask the GM about surroundings, an NPC, or the like. GM must answer honestly.

strengths.name: Loyal
strengths.description: Each AT you spend to help friends gives +2 instead of +1.

strengths.name: Lucky
strengths.description: May spend 2 AT to reroll a stat check.

strengths.name: Prepared
strengths.description: May spend 2 AT to just happen to have one commonplace item with you.

strengths.name: Protective
strengths.description: Add +3 to rolls when defending one of your friends.

strengths.name: Quick Healing
strengths.description: Recover from injuries more quickly. No lasting effects from most injuries.

strengths.name: Rebellious
strengths.description: Add +3 to rolls to persuade or resist persuasion from Kids. Add +3 to resist persuasion from Adults.

strengths.name: Skilled at...
strengths.description: Choose a skill. Assumed to succeed at moderately difficult checks. +3 on harder checks.

strengths.name: Tough
strengths.description: If you lose a combat roll, add +3 to the negative number (still lose but reduce severity).

strengths.name: Treasure Hunter
strengths.description: May spend 1 AT to find a useful item in your surroundings.

strengths.name: Unassuming
strengths.description: May spend 2 AT to not be seen, within reason.

strengths.name: Wealthy
strengths.description: May spend money as though you were in a higher age bracket.

difficultyScale.label: Nearly Impossible
difficultyScale.description: Only the most incredible could succeed.

difficultyScale.label: Incredible
difficultyScale.description: Success would be incredible and impressive.

difficultyScale.label: Extraordinary
difficultyScale.description: Decidedly possible for the truly skilled.

difficultyScale.label: Impressive
difficultyScale.description: Impressive but unsurprising for the skilled.

difficultyScale.label: Moderate
difficultyScale.description: Almost certain for the very skilled.

difficultyScale.label: Easy
difficultyScale.description: Likely for all but the unskilled.

difficultyScale.label: Trivial
difficultyScale.description: Nearly guaranteed except in extreme cases.

statChecks.luckyBreak: Rolling max value on a die triggers a reroll, adding both results together. Can chain.

adversityTokens.spendToBoostRoll: +1 per AT spent (unless Loyal: +2 per AT)
adversityTokens.canIgnoreFear: With GM permission or Heroic strength

npcCreator._comment: Simplified character creation config for audience NPCs at live shows. Not the full character creation flow — just enough for an audience member to build a character in 3-5 minutes on their phone.

fields.label: Character Name
fields.helpText: First name or nickname. Lean into evocative names — there's a big difference between 'Billy' and 'William.'

fields.label: Occupation
fields.placeholder: Bartender, mechanic, high school teacher, sheriff...
fields.helpText: What does your character do? This shapes how they fit into the story.

fields.label: Appearance
fields.placeholder: Describe your character in a sentence or two.
fields.helpText: What do people notice first about your character?

fields.label: One Secret
fields.helpText: Every good character has something they don't want others to know. This stays between you and the GM.

fields.label: Best Stat
fields.helpText: Pick the stat your character is best at. This gets a d20.

fields.label: Worst Stat
fields.helpText: Pick the stat your character is worst at. This gets a d4.

statAssignment._comment: For audience NPCs, we simplify: best stat = d20, worst stat = d4, GM assigns the middle four during play.
statAssignment.remaining: GM assigns d6/d8/d10/d12 during play based on character concept

showConfig._comment: Per-show overrides. This section is specific to The Betawave Tapes: Last Call and would change for each live show (each 'tape').

npcCreatorOverrides.secretPrompt: It's Eddie's going-away party. What are you hiding about your connection to him, or to this town?
